texto: I lived willy-nilly. Without any sense of being part of the order of things. I lived by fragments, pieces, scraps, in the moment, at random, from incident to incident, as if buffeted by ebb and flow. Oftentimes I had the impression that someone had torn the majority of pages out of the book of my life, because they were empty, or because they belonged not to me but to someone else’s life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Wiesław Myśliwski: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I lived willy-nilly. Without any sense of being part of the order of things. I lived by fragments…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, control, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vida e self.
